entities.name: caitanya
entities.cite: sentience in every order of beings

entities.name: vāgrupata
entities.cite: vag-rupata a feature of caitanya as such, not of human acculturation

entities.name: samjñā
entities.cite: it is the samjna, the consciousness or awareness-function, of all transmigrators

entities.name: antas
entities.cite: inward (in thought, in the silent taking-as of 1.124)

entities.name: jāti
entities.cite: every order of beings — down through animals, to whatever stirs

axioms.cite: Wherever there is consciousness at all, there is word-formedness

axioms.name: vivartate
axioms.cite: caitanyam sarva-jatisu, sentience in every order of beings

axioms.name: asraya
axioms.cite: it is the samjna, the consciousness or awareness-function, of all transmigrators

axioms.name: vartate
axioms.cite: operating outward (in speech, in practice) and inward (in thought, in the silent taking-as of 1.124)

axioms.name: avyatikranti
axioms.cite: tan-matram avyatikrantam, does not overstep its measure

denials.name: atikranti
denials.cite: tan-matram avyatikrantam, does not overstep its measure

claims.name: vivartate
claims.cite: This it is that operates as the consciousness of transmigrating beings, without and within
sentience in all the kinds of beings does not step beyond its measure

claims.name: asraya
claims.cite: operates as the consciousness of transmigrating beings

rejected_readings.label: human_cognitive_exclusivity
rejected_readings.rendering: This consciousness operates only in human transmigrating beings, in speech and thought.
rejected_readings.why: Restricts caitanya to humans alone, ignoring the explicit universalization sarvajatisu ('in all kinds of beings'). The commentary requires extension through animals and all sentient orders to establish word-essence as a feature of consciousness as such, not learned language.

rejected_readings.label: consciousness_exceeds_measure
rejected_readings.rendering: Sentience in beings exceeds and transgresses the measure of word-formedness.
rejected_readings.why: Contradicts the core tenet that consciousness does not step beyond the word-measure (tanmatram avyatikrantam). The commentary insists sentience, however dim, remains within word-bounds in all beings.

rejected_readings.label: separation_of_consciousness_and_language
rejected_readings.rendering: The consciousness of beings is independent from and operates separately from any word-essence or linguistic structure.
rejected_readings.why: Asserts independence where the verse and commentary establish coextensiveness and identity. The identity axiom makes this reading's key claim unlicensed.

notes: The verse establishes caitanya (consciousness/sentience) as coextensive with vagrupata (word-essence) across all sentient beings. The identity between consciousness and word-formedness is the core commitment; samjna (awareness) is the cognitive manifestation of this. The measure (tanmatram) that consciousness does not overstep is precisely the word-measure (vagrupata). The pun on samjna (consciousness vs. designation/name) is noted in the contested field; the contract formalizes the upanisadic reading (consciousness) as the dominant commitment.
